zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan CECIL於1510年出生在英國素福爾可斯萬斯 霍爾時，她的父親Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire54歲，母親Alice Dycons34歲。她於從1528年1月11日到1529年1月10日與William Browne在英國賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1573年4月7日在英國英格蘭賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯享年63歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan CECIL于1510年出生在英国素福爾可斯萬斯 霍爾时，她的父亲Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire54岁，母亲Alice Dycons34岁。她于从1528年1月11日到1529年1月10日与William Browne在英国賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1573年4月7日在英国英格兰賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯享年63岁，埋葬在英国英格兰賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: CECIL
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh (Monmouthshire): from the Old Welsh personal name Seisyllt, sometimes wrongly said to derive from the Latin name Caecilius (see Sisley ). The most frequent modern spelling is a Renaissance attempt to connect the surname with Caecilius in the English pronunciation of its time. The Welsh name is more probably, but not definitely, from the Latin name Sextilius, a derivative of sextus ‘sixth’.

History: The great and powerful English Cecil family first came to prominence with David Cecil, a Monmouthshire gentleman who espoused the cause of Henry Tudor and came to court in London after the latter became king in 1485. His grandson William Cecil, Lord Burghley (1520–98), was Elizabeth I's chief adviser for 40 years, and his descendants have remained politically powerful and culturally influential in Britain ever since. They were originally minor Welsh gentry; their name is found in a variety of forms, including Sitsylt, Ceyssel, and Sisseld.

variants.name: Cissell
meanings: English (Middlesex): variant of Welsh   Cecil  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Sisley
meanings: English (Kent): from the popular Middle English female personal name Sisely (Old French Cecile, Cicile, from Latin Caecilia, feminine form of the Roman surname Caecilius, a derivative of caecus ‘blind’). This was the name of a early Christian virgin martyr of the 2nd or 3rd century, who came to be regarded as the patron saint of music. The surname is generally spelt with C- in the Middle Ages, following the trend of the personal name, and with S- in modern times. Compare Cecil and see also Sise , Sisson .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan CECIL was born in 1510, in Swans Hall, Suffolk, England, her father, Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire, was 54 and her mother, Alice Dycons, was 34. She married William Browne from 11 January 1528 to 10 January 1529, in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 7 April 1573, in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 63, and was buried in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan CECIL est née en 1510, à Swans Hall, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire, avait 54 ans et sa mère, Alice Dycons, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé William Browne du 11 janvier 1528 au 10 janvier 1529, à Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 7 avril 1573, à Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterrée à Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan CECIL 1510 in Swans Hall, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire 54 und ihre Mutter Alice Dycons 34. Sie hat von 11. Januar 1528 bis 10. Januar 1529 in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England William Browne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 7. April 1573 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan CECIL è nata il nel 1510 nel Swans Hall, Suffolk, England, sua padre, Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire, aveva 54 anni e sua madre, Alice Dycons, aveva 34 anni. Lei sposato William Browne da 11 gennaio 1528 a 10 gennaio 1529, nel Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 7 aprile 1573, nel Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolta a Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan CECILが1510年にイギリススフォルクスワンス ハルで生まれた時，父Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshireは54歳，母Alice Dyconsは34歳であった。から1528年1月11日まで1529年1月10日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアサウブリジェウオルスでWilliam Browneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1573年4月7日にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアサウブリジェウオルスで63歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアサウブリジェウオルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan CECIL(이)가 1510년 영국 수폴크 스완스 홀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire(은)는 54세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Dycons(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1528년 1월 11일부터 1529년 1월 10일까지 영국 허트퍼드실르 사w브리지워스에서 William Browne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1573년 4월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 사w브리지워스에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 사w브리지워스에 묻혔다.

pt.1528: de 11 de janeiro de 1528 a 10 de janeiro de 1529
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan CECIL nasceu em 1510, em Swans Hall, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire, tinha 54 anos e sua mãe, Alice Dycons, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com William Browne de 11 de janeiro de 1528 a 10 de janeiro de 1529, em Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 7 de abril de 1573, em Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 63 anos, e foi sepultada em Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan CECIL родилась в 1510 г. в местности сванс холл, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire было 54 года, а матери, которую звали Alice Dycons, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Browne с 11 января 1528 по 10 января 1529 в местности савбриджюорт, хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла 7 апреля 1573 г. в местности савбриджюорт, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 63 лет и была похоронена в местности савбриджюорт, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.
